seoTitle: How to Auto-Add a Checklist to a Trello Card
metaDescription: Use Butler automation to automatically attach a pre-written checklist to cards the moment they're created or moved into a list.
intro: Making your team manually recreate the same quality control checklist over and over risks missed steps. This guide covers using Butler automation to instantly stitch a full checklist onto a card the moment it's created or moved into a specific list.

steps.heading: Open the Automation dashboard
steps.body: From your board layout, locate and open the centralized Automation panel.

steps.heading: Create a master template card
steps.body: Build a repository card that houses your baseline checklist configuration.

steps.heading: Set your trigger
steps.body: Define a trigger condition, such as a card being added to a specific list.

steps.heading: Configure the checklist action
steps.body: Set the rule to append the checklist from your template card whenever the trigger fires.

steps.heading: Test the automation
steps.body: Add a test card to confirm the checklist populates as expected.

steps.heading: Fix common errors
steps.body: Check for missing checklist sources or misaligned rules if the automation doesn't run correctly.

faq.question: Where does the checklist template come from?
faq.answer: You create a master template repository card that houses your baseline checklist, which the automation references when it runs.

faq.question: What triggers this kind of automation?
faq.answer: A common trigger is a card being added to a specific list, though you can adjust it to fit your workflow.

faq.question: Why isn't my checklist being added automatically?
faq.answer: Check for a missing checklist source or a misaligned rule, which are the most common causes of execution errors.

faq.question: Will this use up my automation quota?
faq.answer: Yes, each execution counts toward your monthly workspace command limit, so it's worth keeping your rules lean.
